nodes.label: AI as code formatting and restructuring tool
nodes.grounding_span: There is piece of code that needs a better format or requires some restructuring/cleaning

nodes.label: AI as baseline code generator from specific instructions
nodes.label_negative: Writing baseline code from scratch without AI
nodes.grounding_span: I need a baseline code to start with (a blue print) so I request the AI model to built it using highly specific instructions with low ambiguity

nodes.label: AI as quick explanation provider for code logic
nodes.grounding_span: I often require some quick explanation on a code that implements an algorithm, to identify the logic being used in implementation

nodes.label: AI as code translator between languages
nodes.grounding_span: I might need to translate an algorithm I implemented in a certain language into another

nodes.label: AI as initial research tool for science topics
nodes.grounding_span: I also use AI models to perform an initial quick research on a science-related topic, an algorithm, state-of-the-art techniques

nodes.label: AI as bug understanding and suggestion tool
nodes.label_negative: Manual bug understanding without AI suggestions
nodes.grounding_span: I often use the AI-model to understand already-localized bugs, and try variations of the suggestions it provides

nodes.label: Preference for full awareness in deployment code
nodes.label_negative: Delegating deployment code to AI without full awareness
nodes.grounding_span: Something I am creating for deployment and I need to be fully-aware (or as much as possible) of what each part is doing

nodes.label: Preference for self-directed skill building in language proficiency
nodes.label_negative: Using AI to bypass language learning
nodes.grounding_span: I am trying to increase my proficiency in a certain language while analyzing an algorithm

nodes.label: Preference for active learning through source validation
nodes.label_negative: Passive acceptance of AI summaries without validation
nodes.grounding_span: I validate the summary of the research's results provided by the AI model, so that I can actively learn from the source

nodes.label: Collaborative interaction pattern with decreasing specificity
nodes.label_negative: Delegation of entire tasks to AI
nodes.grounding_span: I often collaborate with the model throughout a task. however, the more I advance into the task more specific the questions I pose become

nodes.label: Frustration with unrequested code modifications by AI
nodes.grounding_span: The only possible frustration would be related to situations in which unrequested modifications to a code were made by the AI model

nodes.label: Skill maintenance focused on thinking and specialized debugging
nodes.label_negative: Skill maintenance for tasks fully automatable by AI
nodes.grounding_span: Maintaining such skills is relevant only/mostly if they are connected to a way of thinking, or if the skills are related to areas that will likely need someone who knows enough to debug specialized/challenging issues

nodes.label: AI as enabler of creative work as capability grows
nodes.grounding_span: It could evolve mostly if they become more capable, so that I can do more of the ''creative'' aspect of it

nodes.label: Efficiency and time saving
nodes.grounding_span: I need a baseline code to start with (a blue print) so I request the AI model to built it using highly specific instructions

nodes.label: Understanding and learning

nodes.label: Skill development and proficiency

nodes.label: Active learning from sources
nodes.grounding_span: I validate the summary of the research's results provided by the AI model, so that I can actively learn from the source

nodes.label: Reliability and predictability of AI

nodes.label: Creative expression

nodes.label: Strategic task decomposition — uses AI for specific sub-tasks while retaining control over critical parts

nodes.label: Progressive knowledge absorption — starts with broad collaboration and narrows questions as understanding increases
